result.system_summary: This is an e-commerce web application that allows users to browse products, manage a shopping cart, and place orders. Users interact with product listings, add items to their cart, proceed through checkout, and can review their order history and individual order confirmations.

feature_areas.name: Product browsing
feature_areas.description: Users view a list of available products and their details. This is the entry point for shopping and feeds items into the cart.

test_cases.title: Display all available products
test_cases.description: Verifies that ProductList renders all products returned by GET /products, showing correct name, price, and image for each product.
test_cases.test_data: Three products in the products table with distinct names, prices, categories, and valid image_url values, all with stock_qty > 0.

test_cases.title: Display products from an empty catalogue
test_cases.description: Verifies that ProductList renders a graceful empty state (e.g. 'No products available' message) when GET /products returns an empty array.

test_cases.title: Handle GET /products server error gracefully
test_cases.description: Verifies that when GET /products returns a 500 error, ProductList displays an error message and does not crash.
test_cases.test_data: None required; the API response is mocked to return HTTP 500.

feature_areas.name: Cart management
feature_areas.description: Users add products to their cart, view the current cart contents, and remove individual items. Cart state persists between views.

test_cases.title: Add an in-stock product to the cart
test_cases.description: Verifies that clicking add-to-cart on a ProductCard calls POST /cart/add and the item subsequently appears in GET /cart with the correct quantity.
test_cases.test_data: One product in the products table with stock_qty >= 2; cart_items table empty for the test user.

test_cases.title: Remove an item from the cart
test_cases.description: Verifies that the Cart component calls DELETE /cart/item/:param for the correct product_id and the item no longer appears in the cart after deletion.
test_cases.test_data: One cart_items row for the test user referencing a valid product_id.

test_cases.title: Attempt to add an out-of-stock product to the cart
test_cases.description: Verifies that POST /cart/add rejects the request with a 400/409 error when the target product has stock_qty = 0, and the Cart component displays an appropriate error message.
test_cases.test_data: One product in the products table with stock_qty = 0; cart_items table empty for the test user.

test_cases.title: Attempt to delete a cart item that does not exist
test_cases.description: Verifies that DELETE /cart/item/:param returns a 404 when the product_id is not present in cart_items, and the Cart component handles the error without crashing.
test_cases.test_data: cart_items table empty for the test user; request uses a non-existent product_id.

feature_areas.name: Checkout and order placement
feature_areas.description: Users review their cart contents and submit an order. The checkout process transfers cart items into a new order and clears the cart.

test_cases.title: Successfully place an order with cart items
test_cases.description: Verifies that POST /orders creates a new row in orders, writes corresponding order_items, decrements product stock_qty, and clears cart_items for the user. Checkout component redirects to order confirmation on success.
test_cases.test_data: Two cart_items rows for the test user, each referencing products with stock_qty >= the cart quantity; a valid shipping address supplied in the request body.

test_cases.title: Attempt checkout with an empty cart
test_cases.description: Verifies that POST /orders returns a 400 error when the user's cart_items are empty, and the Checkout component displays a meaningful error rather than creating an empty order.
test_cases.test_data: cart_items table empty for the test user.

test_cases.title: Attempt checkout when a cart item exceeds available stock
test_cases.description: Verifies that POST /orders rejects the order with a 409/400 error if a product's stock_qty has dropped below the requested cart quantity (e.g. due to a concurrent purchase), and no order or order_items rows are created.
test_cases.test_data: One cart_items row for the test user with quantity = 3; corresponding product in products table with stock_qty = 1.

feature_areas.name: Order history and confirmation
feature_areas.description: Users view a list of all their past orders and can drill into an individual order to see its line items and status.

test_cases.title: Display full order history for a user
test_cases.description: Verifies that OrderHistory renders all orders returned by GET /orders, showing correct order ID, status, total_amount, and created_at for each entry.
test_cases.test_data: Three orders in the orders table belonging to the test user with distinct statuses (e.g. 'pending', 'shipped', 'delivered') and total_amount values.

test_cases.title: Display individual order confirmation with line items
test_cases.description: Verifies that OrderConfirmation fetches GET /orders/:param and renders each order_item with correct product name, quantity, and unit_price, plus the order total and shipping address.
test_cases.test_data: One order in the orders table with two associated order_items rows, each referencing distinct products in the products table.

test_cases.title: Request confirmation for a non-existent order ID
test_cases.description: Verifies that GET /orders/:param returns a 404 when the order_id does not exist, and OrderConfirmation displays a user-friendly 'Order not found' message.
test_cases.test_data: orders table empty or containing orders that do not match the requested order_id; request uses a random non-existent UUID/integer.
